diive.pkgs.outlierdetection.stepwiseoutlierdetection¶

OUTLIER DETECTION: STEP-WISE OUTLIER DETECTION¶

This module is part of the diive library: https://github.com/holukas/diive

Classes

StepwiseOutlierDetection(dfin, col, ...[, idstr])

Step-wise outlier detection in time series data
class diive.pkgs.outlierdetection.stepwiseoutlierdetection.StepwiseOutlierDetection(dfin, col, site_lat, site_lon, utc_offset, idstr=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Step-wise outlier detection in time series data

The class is optimized to work in Jupyter notebooks.

Quality flags that can be directly created via this class: - .flag_missingvals_test(): Generate flag that indicates missing records in data - .flag_outliers_abslim_test(): Generate flag that indicates if values in data are outside the specified range - .flag_outliers_abslim_dtnt_test(): Generate flag that indicates if daytime and nighttime values in data are

outside their respectively specified ranges

  • .flag_outliers_increments_zcore_test(): Identify outliers based on the z-score of increments

  • .flag_outliers_localsd_test(): Identify outliers based on the local standard deviation from a running median

  • .flag_manualremoval_test(): Remove data points for range, time or point-by-point

  • .flag_outliers_zscore_dtnt_test(): Identify outliers based on the z-score, separately for daytime and nighttime

  • .flag_outliers_zscore_rolling_test(): Identify outliers based on the rolling z-score

  • .flag_outliers_zscore_test(): Identify outliers based on the z-score

  • .flag_outliers_lof_dtnt_test(): Identify outliers based on local outlier factor, daytime nighttime separately

  • .flag_outliers_lof_test(): Identify outliers based on local outlier factor, across all data

  • .flag_outliers_hampel_dtnt_test(): Identify based on the Hampel filter, daytime nighttime separately

  • .flag_outliers_trim_low_test(): Remove values below threshold and remove an equal amount of records from high end of data

The class is optimized to work in Jupyter notebooks. Various outlier detection methods can be called on-demand. Outlier results are displayed and the user can accept the results and proceed, or repeat the step with adjusted method parameters. An unlimited amount of tests can be chained together.

At the end of the screening, an overall flag (QCF) can be calculated from ALL single flags using the FlagQCF class in diive. The overall flag can then be used to filter the time series.

Screening The stepwise meteoscreening allows to perform step-by-step outlier removal tests on time series data. A preview plot after running a test is shown and the user can decide if results are satisfactory or if the same test with different parameters should be re-run. Once results are satisfactory, the respective test flag is added to the data with .addflag().

Modular structure Due to its modular (step-wise) approach, the stepwise screening can be easily adjusted to work with any type of time series data.

Parameters:

  • dfin (DataFrame)

  • col (str)

  • site_lat (float)

  • site_lon (float)

  • utc_offset (int)

  • idstr (str)

  • dfin (DataFrame)

  • col (str)

  • site_lat (float)

  • site_lon (float)

  • utc_offset (int)

  • idstr (str)

property last_flag: DataFrame¶

Return flag of most recent QC test.

property series_hires_cleaned: Series¶

Return cleaned time series of field(s) as dict of Series

property series_hires_orig: Series¶

Return original time series of field(s) as dict of Series

property flags: DataFrame¶

Return flag(s) as dataframe

showplot_orig(interactive=False)[source]¶

Show original high-resolution data used as input

Parameters:

interactive (bool)

showplot_cleaned(interactive=False)[source]¶

Show current cleaned high-resolution data

Parameters:

interactive (bool)

flag_manualremoval_test(remove_dates, showplot=False, verbose=False)[source]¶

Flag specified records for removal

Parameters:

  • remove_dates (list)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

flag_outliers_zscore_dtnt_test(thres_zscore=4, showplot=False, verbose=False, repeat=True)[source]¶

Flag outliers based on z-score, calculated separately for daytime and nighttime

Parameters:

  • thres_zscore (float)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

  • repeat (bool)

flag_outliers_localsd_test(n_sd=7, winsize=None, showplot=False, constant_sd=False, separate_daytime_nighttime=False, verbose=False, repeat=True)[source]¶

Identify outliers based on standard deviation in a rolling window

Parameters:

  • n_sd (float | list)

  • winsize (int | list)

  • showplot (bool)

  • constant_sd (bool)

  • separate_daytime_nighttime (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

  • repeat (bool)

flag_outliers_increments_zcore_test(thres_zscore=30, showplot=False, verbose=False, repeat=True)[source]¶

Identify outliers based on the z-score of record increments

Parameters:

  • thres_zscore (int)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

  • repeat (bool)

flag_outliers_trim_low_test(trim_daytime=False, trim_nighttime=False, lower_limit=None, showplot=False, verbose=False)[source]¶

Flag values below a given absolute limit as outliers, then flag an equal number of datapoints at the high end as outliers.

Parameters:

  • trim_daytime (bool)

  • trim_nighttime (bool)

  • lower_limit (float)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

flag_outliers_hampel_dtnt_test(window_length=13, n_sigma=5.5, n_sigma_daytime=None, n_sigma_nighttime=None, k=1.4826, use_differencing=True, separate_day_night=True, showplot=False, verbose=False, repeat=True)[source]¶

Identify outliers in a sliding window based on the Hampel filter, separately for daytime and nighttime data.

Parameters:

  • window_length (int)

  • n_sigma (float)

  • n_sigma_daytime (float)

  • n_sigma_nighttime (float)

  • k (float)

  • use_differencing (bool)

  • separate_day_night (bool)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

  • repeat (bool)

flag_outliers_zscore_test(thres_zscore=4, showplot=False, verbose=False, plottitle=None, repeat=True)[source]¶

Identify outliers based on the z-score of records

Parameters:

  • thres_zscore (int)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

  • plottitle (str)

  • repeat (bool)

flag_outliers_zscore_rolling_test(thres_zscore=4, showplot=False, verbose=False, plottitle=None, repeat=True, winsize=None)[source]¶

Identify outliers based on the z-score of records

Parameters:

  • thres_zscore (float)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

  • repeat (bool)

  • winsize (int)

flag_outliers_lof_dtnt_test(n_neighbors=None, contamination=None, showplot=False, verbose=False, repeat=True, n_jobs=1)[source]¶

Local outlier factor, separately for daytime and nighttime data

Parameters:

  • n_neighbors (int)

  • contamination (float)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

  • repeat (bool)

  • n_jobs (int)

Local outlier factor, across all data

Parameters:

  • n_neighbors (int)

  • contamination (float)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

  • repeat (bool)

  • n_jobs (int)

flag_outliers_abslim_dtnt_test(daytime_minmax, nighttime_minmax, showplot=False, verbose=False)[source]¶

Identify outliers based on absolute limits separately for daytime and nighttime

Parameters:

  • daytime_minmax (list[float, float])

  • nighttime_minmax (list[float, float])

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

flag_outliers_abslim_test(minval, maxval, showplot=False, verbose=False)[source]¶

Identify outliers based on absolute limits

Parameters:

  • minval (float)

  • maxval (float)

  • showplot (bool)

  • verbose (bool)

addflag()[source]¶

Add flag of most recent test to data and update filtered series that will be used to continue with the next test.
